title.zh: 连词（并列、选择、转折、因果、从属）

summary.en: Conjunctions link words, phrases, and clauses to add logic. English has two big families: coordinating conjunctions join grammatically equal parts (parallel, alternative, contrast, cause-effect), and subordinating conjunctions attach a clause to a main clause.
summary.zh: 连词把语句连接起来、增加逻辑性。英语连词分两大类：并列连词连接语法地位同等的词、词组或句子（并列、选择、转折、因果），从属连词把从句连接到主句上。

heading.en: What a conjunction is — two big families

body.en: A conjunction links language to make it more logical — like Chinese 然后/但是/因此. One family, coordinating conjunctions, joins words, phrases, or clauses that share equal grammatical standing. The other family, subordinating conjunctions, joins a main clause to a subordinate (dependent) clause. Since Chinese has no clauses like English does, subordinating conjunctions deserve special attention.
body.zh: 连词把语句连接起来、增加逻辑性，就像中文的“然后、但是、因此”。一类是并列连词，连接语法地位同等的单词、词组或句子；另一类是从属连词，连接主句和从句。因为中文没有英文那样的从句，从属连词需要特别注意。

concepts.sourceRef: 英语连词分两大类：并列连词和从属连词

heading.en: Coordinating — parallel relation (and, both...and, not only...but also)
heading.zh: 并列连词——并列关系（and / both...and / not only...but also）

body.en: The simplest coordinating logic just adds things together: 'and', 'both...and', and 'not only...but also'. The joined parts can be single words, phrases, or whole clauses, and they stay grammatically equal.
body.zh: 最容易理解的并列逻辑就是把事物加在一起：and、both...and、not only...but also。连接的可以是单词、词组或句子，且它们语法地位同等。

body.en: The parallel relation can also be negated. 'Neither...nor' adds two things in the negative — neither one nor the other.
body.zh: 并列关系还可以表否定。neither...nor 把两样东西在否定意义上加起来——既不是这个也不是那个。

heading.zh: 并列连词——选择关系（or / either...or）

body.en: Don't think the alternative meaning clashes with the label 'coordinating': 'coordinating' refers to joining equal grammatical units, while the logic expressed can be a choice. 'Or' and 'either...or' offer a choice; its negative form is 'neither...nor' (neither ate nor slept), and 'or' can also warn ('hurry, or you'll be late').
body.zh: 千万别以为选择关系和并列连词冲突——并列连词指的是连接同等语法地位，而它表达的逻辑可以是选择。or 和 either...or 表示选择；其否定是 neither...nor（既没吃也没睡），or 还可以表警告（快点，否则你要迟到了）。

body.en: With 'either...or', 'neither...nor', and 'not only...but also', the verb agrees with the subject nearest to it. In 'Either the rabbit or I am going', the verb is 'am' because 'I' is closest. This is the proximity rule.
body.zh: 用 either...or、neither...nor、not only...but also 时，谓语动词由离它最近的主语决定。在“Either the rabbit or I am going”中用 am，因为“I”离得最近。这就是就近原则。

heading.en: Coordinating — contrast relation (but, yet, however, nevertheless)
heading.zh: 并列连词——转折关系（but / yet / however / nevertheless）

body.en: Contrast is the '虽然...但是' idea. 'But' can be replaced by 'yet' (the conjunction 'yet' meaning 'but', not the adverb 'yet' meaning 'not yet'). 'However' and 'nevertheless' also mark a turn that negates the information just mentioned; 'nevertheless' is more formal and sounds bookish in speech.
body.zh: 转折就是“虽然...但是”。but 可以用 yet 替换（这里 yet 是表转折的连词，不是表“还没有”的副词）。however 和 nevertheless 也标转折，否定刚才提到的信息；nevertheless 偏书面，口语里听起来文绉绉。

heading.zh: 并列连词——因果关系（so / therefore / for）

body.en: For cause and effect, 'so' (replaceable by the more formal 'therefore') puts the cause before it and the result after. 'For' reverses the order — result first, then cause — and here 'for' is the conjunction meaning 'because', not the preposition meaning 'for the purpose of'.
body.zh: 表因果时，so（可用更书面的 therefore 替换）连词前是原因、后是结果；for 则相反——前果后因，这里的 for 是表“因为”的连词，不是表“为了”的介词。

heading.en: Subordinating — links a main clause to a clause
heading.zh: 从属连词——连接主句和从句

body.en: 'Because' is missing from the coordinating list because it is a subordinating conjunction, not a coordinating one — so it cannot pair with the coordinating 'so'. Subordinating conjunctions fall into two groups: those introducing noun clauses (subject, object, predicative, appositive) such as 'that', 'if', 'whether', and those introducing adverbial clauses — time (when, while, before, after, until, since), condition (if, unless), concession (although, though, even if), reason (because, since, as), and more.
body.zh: because 不在并列连词里，因为它是从属连词，不是并列连词——所以它不能和并列连词 so 连用。从属连词分两类：引导名词性从句（主语、宾语、表语、同位语从句）的，如 that、if、whether；引导状语从句的，如时间（when, while, before, after, until, since）、条件（if, unless）、让步（although, though, even if）、原因（because, since, as）等。

examples.text: The rabbit is smart and cute.

note.en: 'And' joins two equal adjectives — the parallel relation. 这兔子聪明且可爱。
note.zh: and 连接两个同等的形容词——并列关系。这兔子聪明且可爱。

examples.text: The rabbit not only teaches English but also makes videos.

note.en: 'Not only...but also' adds two parallel predicates. 兔子不仅教英语，还做视频。
note.zh: not only...but also 把两个并列的谓语加在一起。兔子不仅教英语，还做视频。

examples.text: The rabbit likes neither apples nor pears.

note.en: Negative parallel: 'neither...nor'. 这兔子既不喜欢苹果，也不喜欢梨子。
note.zh: 否定的并列：neither...nor。这兔子既不喜欢苹果，也不喜欢梨子。

examples.text: Does the rabbit like apples or carrots?

note.en: 'Or' offers a choice — the alternative relation. 兔子喜欢什么，苹果还是胡萝卜？
note.zh: or 表示选择——选择关系。兔子喜欢什么，苹果还是胡萝卜？

note.en: 'Or' can also warn of a consequence. 快点，否则你要迟到了。
note.zh: or 也可以表示警告后果。快点，否则你要迟到了。

examples.text: Either the rabbit or I am going to the party tonight.

note.en: Proximity rule: the verb is 'am' because 'I' is the nearest subject. 不是兔子就是我将参加今晚的聚会。
note.zh: 就近原则：用 am，因为最近的主语是“I”。不是兔子就是我将参加今晚的聚会。

examples.text: I haven't eaten yet, but I'm not hungry yet.

note.en: Here 'but' marks contrast; both 'yet' words are adverbs meaning 'not yet', NOT the conjunction. 我还没吃，但我还不饿。
note.zh: 这里 but 表转折；两个 yet 都是表“还没有”的副词，不是连词。我还没吃，但我还不饿。

examples.text: The carrots were ready, however, the rabbit was still making videos.

note.en: 'However' (or the more formal 'nevertheless') negates the prior information — a contrast. 胡萝卜做好了，但是兔子还在做视频。
note.zh: however（或更书面的 nevertheless）否定前面的信息——转折。胡萝卜做好了，但是兔子还在做视频。

examples.text: The rabbit is busy, so he won't go to the party tonight.

note.en: Cause before 'so', result after. 'Therefore' could replace 'so' (more formal). 兔子很忙，所以今晚他不能参加聚会了。
note.zh: so 前是原因，后是结果。可用更书面的 therefore 替换。兔子很忙，所以今晚他不能参加聚会了。

examples.text: The rabbit will skip lunch, for he is busy making a video.

note.en: 'For' here is the conjunction 'because' (result first, then cause), not the preposition. 兔子不吃午餐了，因为他在忙着做视频。
note.zh: 这里 for 是表“因为”的连词（前果后因），不是介词。兔子不吃午餐了，因为他在忙着做视频。

examples.text: I saw that the rabbit ate the carrot.

note.en: 'That' is a subordinating conjunction introducing an object clause (it only connects, no meaning). 我看见兔子吃了胡萝卜。
note.zh: that 是引导宾语从句的从属连词（只起连接作用，无实义）。我看见兔子吃了胡萝卜。

examples.text: The rabbit will skip lunch because he is busy making a video.

annotations.quote: he is busy making a video

note.en: 'Because' is a SUBORDINATING conjunction (reason adverbial clause) — so it can't pair with the coordinating 'so'. 兔子不吃午餐，因为他在忙着做视频。
note.zh: because 是从属连词（原因状语从句）——所以不能和并列连词 so 连用。兔子不吃午餐，因为他在忙着做视频。

prompt.en: Which logical relation does 'The rabbit ate neither apples nor pears' express?
prompt.zh: “The rabbit ate neither apples nor pears”表达哪种逻辑关系？

explain.en: 'Neither...nor' adds two items in the negative — a negative parallel relation, still a coordinating conjunction.
explain.zh: neither...nor 在否定意义上把两样东西加起来——否定的并列关系，仍属并列连词。

prompt.en: Proximity rule — fill the verb: 'Either the rabbit or I ____ going to the party.'
prompt.zh: 就近原则——填动词：“Either the rabbit or I ____ going to the party.”

explain.en: With 'either...or', the verb agrees with the nearest subject 'I', so it is 'am'.
explain.zh: 用 either...or 时谓语动词由最近的主语“I”决定，所以是 am。

prompt.en: In 'The rabbit will skip lunch, for he is busy', what is 'for'?
prompt.zh: 在“The rabbit will skip lunch, for he is busy”中，“for”是什么？

text.en: A preposition meaning 'for the purpose of'

explain.en: Here 'for' is the coordinating conjunction meaning 'because' (result first, then cause), not the preposition.
explain.zh: 这里 for 是表“因为”的并列连词（前果后因），不是介词。

quizzes.sentence: Because the rabbit is busy, so he won't go to the party.
quizzes.fix: Because the rabbit is busy, he won't go to the party.

explain.en: 'Because' is a subordinating conjunction; it cannot pair with the coordinating 'so' in the same sentence. Drop 'so'.
explain.zh: because 是从属连词，不能在同一句里和并列连词 so 连用。去掉 so。

prompt.en: Which word is a SUBORDINATING conjunction?

explain.en: 'Although' introduces a concession (adverbial) clause — a subordinating conjunction. 'But' and 'or' are coordinating.
explain.zh: although 引导让步状语从句——从属连词。but 和 or 是并列连词。

prompt.en: Fill the contrast conjunction (the formal, bookish one starting with N): 'The carrots were ready; ____, the rabbit was still making videos.'
prompt.zh: 填表转折的连词（书面、文绉绉、以 N 开头的那个）：“The carrots were ready; ____, the rabbit was still making videos.”

explain.en: 'Nevertheless' negates the prior information to mark a contrast; it is more formal than 'however' and sounds bookish in speech.
explain.zh: nevertheless 否定前面的信息以标转折；比 however 更书面，口语里听起来文绉绉。

title.en: Why the rabbit skips the party

lines.speaker: Wolf
lines.text: The rabbit is smart and cute, but he's always busy.

note.zh: 先并列（and）再转折（but）——都是并列连词。

lines.speaker: Rabbit
lines.text: The carrots were ready, so I should eat, however, I'm still making videos.

lines.speaker: Wolf
lines.text: So either the rabbit or I am going tonight?

note.en: Alternative relation; 'am' follows the proximity rule (nearest subject 'I').
note.zh: 选择关系；am 遵循就近原则（最近的主语是“I”）。

lines.speaker: Rabbit
lines.text: You go, because I am busy making a video.

annotations.quote: I am busy making a video

note.en: 'Because' is subordinating — note we don't add 'so'.
note.zh: because 是从属连词——注意不能再加 so。
